themes.label: Central banks and interest rates
themes.why_it_matters: Rates and liquidity drive borrowing costs, valuations, and currencies.

themes.label: Inflation, wages, and cost pressures
themes.why_it_matters: Inflation shapes real incomes, margins, and policy expectations.

themes.label: Growth, recession, and labour market
themes.why_it_matters: Growth and labour data alter recession risk and earnings expectations.

themes.label: Energy and commodities
themes.why_it_matters: Commodity shocks feed into inflation, trade balances, and supply chains.

themes.label: Trade policy, tariffs, sanctions, and export controls
themes.why_it_matters: Trade restrictions reshape supply chains and market access.

themes.label: Technology, AI, chips, and industrial policy
themes.why_it_matters: Tech policy shapes competitiveness, capex cycles, and strategic autonomy.

themes.label: Financial stability, credit, and banking stress
themes.why_it_matters: Financial stress can spill quickly into the real economy.

themes.label: Fiscal policy, budgets, and sovereign debt
themes.why_it_matters: Budget choices affect demand, borrowing costs, and sector winners.

themes.label: Wars, security risks, and strategic chokepoints
themes.why_it_matters: Security shocks can disrupt trade and raise commodity prices.

themes.label: Supply chains, shipping, and logistics
themes.why_it_matters: Logistics disruption changes delivery times, inventory costs, and pricing.
